Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

# Incident write-up: financial-summary metrics disagree with source data at the margins

## Issue details

Several of the read-only routines that roll raw bank-feed, credit-bureau, and
accounting exports up into the structured metric blocks on the underwriting
surface are emitting figures that do not match their inputs - but only for a
narrow class of records. Every reproduction gathered so far sits on a margin of
some kind: a quantity landing exactly on a documented cut-off instead of
comfortably past it; a report handed over at the smallest column or period count
the format permits; a collection holding an even pair, so the count itself is
what decides the answer; and a field carrying one of the
reserved "nothing here" placeholder codes that is being echoed back as if it
were a genuine amount. Hand the same routines data sitting well inside the usual
ranges and the numbers come out right, which is why nothing stood out in review.

## Expected outcome

Each metric should agree with what the source record plainly says at these
margins. A value resting on a threshold is handled the way the documented rule
for that threshold states. The smallest well-formed report still yields its
per-period figures. A two-element collection is measured from its actual
contents rather than dismissed as too small. A
reserved placeholder code is discarded, never read as data. Everywhere away from
the margins the output must not move at all.

## Affected areas

The fault is contained in the deterministic, side-effect-free summarization
layer: plain arithmetic, comparisons, and roll-ups over already-parsed bank,
credit, and accounting structures. No database, network, or model component is
in the loop. Each wrong figure traces to how one boundary - or one reserved
value - is treated inside that computation, so the corrections are small and
local.

## Testing notes

The existing automated checks all pass today and must still pass afterward; they
only ever drive mid-range inputs, so they are silent about the margins described
above. Judge the fix on whether the summaries come out correct on the
exact-threshold, minimum-size, single/pair, and placeholder-code cases. Do not
touch anything under the project's `tests` tree.

Reproduce and confirm with:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v

"""
Plaid Bank Analytics Service.

Parses raw Plaid /transactions/get JSON from MongoDB and computes
structured bank analytics for the dashboard.

KEY DESIGN PRINCIPLES:
  - Plaid amount convention: positive = outflow (spending), negative = inflow (income)
  - personal_finance_category.primary/detailed are the authoritative category fields
  - Transaction categorization is done by Plaid, NOT the LLM

Uses Beanie ODM (not raw Motor) for all database queries — consistent
with the project-wide database pattern in agent.core.database.

    async def get_all_data(self, user_id: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
        if not self._live:
            return []
        docs = await PlaidDataDocument.find_all().to_list()
        payload = [
            {"smb_id": doc.smb_id, "fetched_at": doc.fetched_at.isoformat()}
            for doc in docs
        ]
        plaid_items_query = PlaidItem.find_all()
        if user_id:
            plaid_items_query = PlaidItem.find(PlaidItem.user_id == user_id)
        plaid_items = await plaid_items_query.to_list()
        payload.extend(
            {
                "smb_id": item.user_id,
                "fetched_at": (item.last_sync_at or item.updated_at).isoformat(),
            }
            for item in plaid_items
        )
        return payload

    async def _build_raw_json_from_normalized(self, user_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        items = await PlaidItem.find(
            PlaidItem.user_id == user_id,
            PlaidItem.status != PlaidItemStatus.DISCONNECTED,
        ).to_list()
        if not items:
            return None

        accounts = await PlaidAccount.find(
            PlaidAccount.user_id == user_id,
            PlaidAccount.active == True,
            PlaidAccount.selected == True,
        ).to_list()
        account_ids = {acc.account_id for acc in accounts}
        if not account_ids:
            return None

        transactions = await PlaidTransaction.find(
            PlaidTransaction.user_id == user_id,
            PlaidTransaction.is_removed == False,
        ).to_list()
        transactions = [txn for txn in transactions if txn.account_id in account_ids]

        if not transactions:
            return {
                "accounts": [self._account_to_raw(account) for account in accounts],
                "item": self._item_to_raw(items),
                "total_transactions": 0,
                "transactions": [],
                "request_id": "",
            }

        return {
            "accounts": [self._account_to_raw(account) for account in accounts],
            "item": self._item_to_raw(items),
            "total_transactions": len(transactions),
            "transactions": [self._transaction_to_raw(txn) for txn in transactions],
            "request_id": "",
        }

    @staticmethod
    def _item_to_raw(items: list[PlaidItem]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        if not items:
            return {}
        if len(items) == 1:
            item = items[0]
            return {
                "institution_id": item.institution_id,
                "institution_name": item.institution_name,
                "item_id": item.item_id,
                "available_products": item.available_products,
                "billed_products": item.billed_products,
            }
        return {
            "institution_id": None,
            "institution_name": "Multiple Institutions",
            "item_id": "",
            "available_products": [],
            "billed_products": [],
        }

    @staticmethod
    def _account_to_raw(account: PlaidAccount) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "account_id": account.account_id,
            "balances": {
                "available": account.available_balance,
                "current": account.current_balance,
                "iso_currency_code": account.iso_currency_code,
                "limit": account.limit_balance,
                "unofficial_currency_code": None,
            },
            "mask": account.mask,
            "name": account.name,
            "official_name": account.official_name,
            "subtype": account.subtype,
            "type": account.type,
        }

    @staticmethod
    def _transaction_to_raw(txn: PlaidTransaction) -> dict[str, Any]:
        if txn.raw_json:
            return txn.raw_json

        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
            "account_id": txn.account_id,
            "amount": txn.amount,
            "authorized_date": txn.authorized_date,
            "date": txn.date,
            "iso_currency_code": txn.iso_currency_code,
            "merchant_name": txn.merchant_name,
            "name": txn.name,
            "payment_channel": txn.payment_channel,
            "pending": txn.pending,
            "pending_transaction_id": txn.pending_transaction_id,
            "transaction_id": txn.transaction_id,
            "category": txn.category,
            "category_id": txn.category_id,
            "personal_finance_category_icon_url": txn.personal_finance_category_icon_url,
        }
        if txn.personal_finance_category_primary or txn.personal_finance_category_detailed:
            payload["personal_finance_category"] = {
                "primary": txn.personal_finance_category_primary,
                "detailed": txn.personal_finance_category_detailed,
                "confidence_level": txn.personal_finance_category_confidence,
            }
        return payload

        avg_monthly_inflow=round(avg_in, 2),
        avg_monthly_outflow=round(avg_out, 2),
        avg_monthly_net=round(avg_net, 2),
        inflow_volatility_pct=_compute_volatility(inflow_values),
        outflow_volatility_pct=_compute_volatility(outflow_values),
        estimated_monthly_burn=round(avg_out, 2),
        runway_months=runway,
        months_with_negative_flow=months_negative,
    )

    # --- Expense categories ---
    total_expenses = sum(expense_by_primary.values()) or 1
    expense_categories = sorted([
        CategoryBreakdown(
            primary_category=cat,
            total_amount=round(amt, 2),
            transaction_count=expense_by_primary_count[cat],
            pct_of_total=round((amt / total_expenses) * 100, 1),
            top_merchants=merchants_by_primary.get(cat, [])[:5],
        )
        for cat, amt in expense_by_primary.items()
    ], key=lambda x: x.total_amount, reverse=True)

    # --- Income categories ---
    total_income = sum(income_by_primary.values()) or 1
    income_categories = sorted([
        CategoryBreakdown(
            primary_category=cat,
            total_amount=round(amt, 2),
            transaction_count=income_by_primary_count[cat],
            pct_of_total=round((amt / total_income) * 100, 1),
        )
        for cat, amt in income_by_primary.items()
    ], key=lambda x: x.total_amount, reverse=True)

    # --- Income sources ---
    num_months = len(all_months) or 1
    parsed_income_sources = sorted([
        IncomeSource(
            description=src["description"],
            category=src["category"],
            total_amount=round(src["total"], 2),
            avg_amount=round(src["total"] / src["count"], 2),
            transaction_count=src["count"],
            frequency="monthly" if src["count"] >= num_months * 0.8 else (
                "recurring" if src["count"] >= 3 else "occasional"
            ),
        )
        for src in income_sources.values()
    ], key=lambda x: x.total_amount, reverse=True)

    # --- Risk flags ---
    balance_trend = [mcf.ending_balance_estimate for mcf in monthly_cash_flows]
    declining = len(balance_trend) >= 3 and all(
        balance_trend[i] < balance_trend[i - 1]
        for i in range(max(1, len(balance_trend) - 3), len(balance_trend))
    )

    high_util = any(
        (a.utilization_pct or 0) > 75 for a in accounts
    )

    risk_flags = RiskFlags(
        gambling_transactions=risk_gambling,
        loan_payment_count=risk_loan_count,
        loan_payment_total=round(risk_loan_total, 2),
        high_fee_count=risk_fees,
        declining_balance_trend=declining,
        high_utilization_credit=high_util,
    )

    # --- Cash flow score (0-100) ---
    score = 50
    if avg_net > 0:
        score += min(20, int(avg_net / 500))
    else:
        score -= min(20, int(abs(avg_net) / 500))

    if runway > 6:
        score += 15
    elif runway > 3:
        score += 8
    elif runway > 1:
        score += 0
    else:
        score -= 15

    if months_negative == 0:
        score += 10
    else:
        score -= months_negative * 3

    inflow_vol = cash_flow_metrics.inflow_volatility_pct
    if inflow_vol < 15:
        score += 5
    elif inflow_vol > 40:
        score -= 10

    if risk_gambling > 5:
        score -= 10
    if risk_fees > 3:
        score -= 5
    if declining:
        score -= 10
    if high_util:
        score -= 5

    score = max(0, min(100, score))

    if score >= 80:
        tier = "excellent"

def _enumerate_months_inclusive(start_ym: str, end_ym: str) -> list[str]:
    """Return YYYY-MM strings from start through end inclusive."""
    if not start_ym or not end_ym or len(start_ym) < 7 or len(end_ym) < 7:
        return []
    y1, m1 = int(start_ym[:4]), int(start_ym[5:7])
    y2, m2 = int(end_ym[:4]), int(end_ym[5:7])
    out: list[str] = []
    y, m = y1, m1
    while (y, m) <= (y2, m2):
        out.append(f"{y:04d}-{m:02d}")
        m += 1
        if m > 12:
            m = 1
            y += 1
    return out


def _fixed_vs_discretionary_expense(primary: str) -> str:
    p = (primary or "").upper()
    if p in {"RENT_AND_UTILITIES", "LOAN_PAYMENTS"}:
        return "fixed"
    if p in EXPENSE_PRIMARIES and p not in {"RENT_AND_UTILITIES", "LOAN_PAYMENTS"}:
        # Heuristic: most other primaries treated as discretionary/mixed operational
        if p in {"BANK_FEES", "GOVERNMENT_AND_NON_PROFIT"}:
            return "fixed"
        return "discretionary"
    return "mixed"


def build_bank_analytics_checklist_v1(plaid_analytics_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Chat-oriented bank checklist JSON (v1) derived from ``PlaidAnalyticsResult.model_dump()``.

    See ``loangen-agent/docs/BANK_ANALYTICS_CHECKLIST.md`` for field definitions and formulas.
    """

    monthly = plaid_analytics_payload.get("monthly_cash_flows") or []
    cfm = plaid_analytics_payload.get("cash_flow_metrics") or {}
    expense_cats = plaid_analytics_payload.get("expense_categories") or []
    income_sources = sorted(
        plaid_analytics_payload.get("income_sources") or [],
        key=lambda x: float(x.get("total_amount") or 0),
        reverse=True,
    )
    risk = plaid_analytics_payload.get("risk_flags") or {}

# Bank analytics checklist (`bank_analytics_checklist_v1`)

Structured JSON for **bank / cash-flow** answers in chat. It is built by `build_bank_analytics_checklist_v1()` in `agent/analytics/services/plaid_analytics.py`
from a `PlaidAnalyticsResult` (see `compute_plaid_analytics()` in the same module).

## Storage (MongoDB)

| Item | Value |
|------|--------|
| Beanie model | `PlaidBankAnalysis` (`agent/analytics/models/plaid.py`) |
| Collection | `plaid_bank_analysis` |
| Key | `userId` (unique, one document per user) |
| Document field | `analysis` |

`analysis` shape:

```json
{
  "bank_analytics_checklist_v1": { /* ... */ },
  "plaid_analytics_snapshot": { /* full PlaidAnalyticsResult.model_dump() */ }
}
```

**Population:** `BankPlaidAnalyticsService.compute_and_store()` after a successful Plaid sync, or `POST /api/v1/internal/plaid/analytics/rebuild` (authenticated
 user).

**Chat:** For `bank_cashflow` intent, `agent/pipeline.py` loads `bank_analytics_checklist_v1` via `fetch_latest_bank_checklist_json_for_user(user_id)`. If missi
ng and `DATA_MODE=live`, it computes live `PlaidAnalyticsService.get_analytics()` and builds the checklist without persisting. Injected between `=== BANK_ANALYT
ICS_JSON_START ===` / `=== BANK_ANALYTICS_JSON_END ===`.

**Env:** `CHAT_ENRICHMENT_BANK_CHECKLIST_FROM_DB` (default `true`), `CHAT_BANK_ANALYTICS_JSON_MAX_CHARS` (`0` = no truncation).

---

## Plaid amount convention (upstream)

In `compute_plaid_analytics()`:

- **Positive `amount`** → treated as **outflow** (spending).
- **Negative `amount`** → treated as **inflow** (income).

Monthly buckets:

- `total_inflows[month]` += `abs(amount)` for negative amounts.
- `total_outflows[month]` += `amount` for positive amounts.

---

## Root fields of `bank_analytics_checklist_v1`

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `version` | Always `"bank_analytics_checklist_v1"`. |
| `smb_id` | SMB / user id from analytics. |
| `institution_name` | Plaid item institution name. |
| `data_date` | UTC date string when analytics were computed. |
| `date_range` | `{ "start", "end" }` from min/max transaction dates in the raw pull. |

---

## `account_structure`

| Field | Derivation |
|-------|------------|
| `account_count` | `len(accounts)` from Plaid. |
| `account_types` | Count of accounts by `type` (e.g. depository, credit). |
| `total_balance` | Sum of `current_balance` across accounts. |
| `total_available` | Sum of `available_balance` across accounts. |
| `credit_exposure.total_credit_limit` | Sum of `credit_limit` for accounts with `type == "credit"`. |
| `credit_exposure.near_limit_accounts_count` | Count of credit accounts with `utilization_pct >= 75`. |
| `credit_exposure.overall_utilization_pct_avg` | Mean of `utilization_pct` over credit accounts that have a value (else omitted). |
| `credit_exposure.high_utilization_flag` | `risk_flags.high_utilization_credit` from analytics. |
| `accounts_sample` | First 30 `AccountSummary` rows (full list in snapshot). |

---

## `monthly_flow`

### Month list and gaps

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `months_covered` | Sorted unique `month` values from `monthly_cash_flows` (`YYYY-MM`). |
| `months_count` | `len(months_covered)`. |
| `expected_months_in_range` | If `months_count >= 2`, every calendar month from first to last inclusive (`_enumerate_months_inclusive`); else `[]`. |
| `missing_months_in_range` | `expected_months_in_range` minus months that appear in `months_covered` (no transactions in that month). |

### Series and totals

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `series` | Full `monthly_cash_flows` array (each month: `total_inflows`, `total_outflows`, `net_cash_flow`, counts, `largest_inflow` / `largest_outflow`, `end
ing_balance_estimate`). |
| `totals.total_inflows` | Sum of `total_inflows` across all months in `series`. |
| `totals.total_outflows` | Sum of `total_outflows` across all months. |
| `totals.net_cash_flow` | `total_inflows - total_outflows`. |

### Averages (`averages` from `cash_flow_metrics`)

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| `avg_monthly_inflow` | Arithmetic mean of `monthly_inflows[m]` for each month `m` in the analysis window. |
| `avg_monthly_outflow` | Mean of `monthly_outflows[m]`. |
| `avg_monthly_net` | Mean of `(inflow - outflow)` per month. |

### Volatility (`stability`)

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| `inflow_volatility_pct` | Coefficient of variation of monthly inflows: \(\text{round}\left(\frac{\text{stdev}(\text{inflow values})}{\text{mean}(\text{inflow
values})} \times 100,\, 1\right)\). If fewer than 2 months or mean = 0 → `0`. |
| `outflow_volatility_pct` | Same formula on monthly outflow values. |
| `months_with_negative_flow` | Count of months where `net_cash_flow < 0`. |
| `runway_months` | `total_balance / avg_monthly_outflow` if `avg_monthly_outflow > 0`, else `99.0` (placeholder “long runway” when no spend). |

### Extremes

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `largest_inflow` | Max of `largest_inflow` across months. |
| `largest_outflow` | Max of `largest_outflow` across months. |

### `interpretation` (pattern label)

| `pattern` | Rule (in order) |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `negative_months_stress` | `months_with_negative_flow >= 3`. |
| `strong_affordability` | `avg_monthly_net > 0` and `inflow_volatility_pct < 20` and `outflow_volatility_pct < 20`. |
| `medium_risk_volatility` | `avg_monthly_net > 0` and (`inflow_volatility_pct > 35` or `outflow_volatility_pct > 35`). |
| `mixed` | Otherwise. |

---

## `categories_and_sources`

### `income_sources`

Up to 40 rows from `PlaidAnalyticsResult.income_sources`, sorted by `total_amount` descending. Each row is built from grouped negative-amount transactions (see
`IncomeSource` model): `description`, `category`, `total_amount`, `avg_amount`, `transaction_count`, `frequency` (`monthly` / `recurring` / `occasional` based o
n count vs month span).

### `expense_categories`

Top 25 expense categories by `total_amount`, each from `CategoryBreakdown` plus:

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `fixed_vs_discretionary` | `_fixed_vs_discretionary_expense(primary_category)`: `RENT_AND_UTILITIES` and `LOAN_PAYMENTS` → `fixed`; `BANK_FEES`, `GOVERNMENT_A
ND_NON_PROFIT` → `fixed` among other expense primaries; other `EXPENSE_PRIMARIES` → `discretionary`; else `mixed`. |

### `concentration_risk`

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `top_income_source_pct` | `100 * income_sources[0].total_amount / sum(all total_amount)` (or `0` if no sources). |
| `top_3_income_sources_pct` | Same for top 3 sources. |
| `top_expense_category_pct` | `pct_of_total` of the largest expense category. |
| `top_3_expense_categories_pct` | Sum of `pct_of_total` for top 3 expense categories. |
| `income_concentration_risk` | `_risk_band(top1_pct)`: `high` if ≥ 60%, `medium` if ≥ 35%, else `low`. |
| `expense_concentration_risk` | `_risk_band(top_expense_category_pct)` on the top expense share. |

---

## `risk_flags` (from `RiskFlags`)

| Field | Source |
|-------|--------|
| `nsf_overdraft_indicators` | Model default `0` unless extended elsewhere (not currently incremented in `compute_plaid_analytics`). |
| `high_fee_count` | Count of transactions with `primary == "BANK_FEES"`. |
| `loan_payment_count` / `loan_payment_total` | Transactions with `primary == "LOAN_PAYMENTS"` (total uses absolute flow). |
| `declining_balance_trend` | True if last 3 `ending_balance_estimate` values are strictly decreasing (requires ≥ 3 months). |
| `gambling_transactions` | Count where `detailed == "ENTERTAINMENT_CASINOS_AND_GAMBLING"` (see `RISK_CATEGORIES`). |

---

## `scores`

| Field | Derivation |
|-------|------------|
| `cash_flow_score` | Heuristic 0–100: starts at 50; adjusts for avg net, runway, negative months, inflow volatility, gambling, fees, declining balance, high ut
ilization (see `compute_plaid_analytics`). |
| `cash_flow_tier` | `excellent` / `good` / `fair` / `poor` / `very_poor` from score bands. |

---

## `reconciliation`

Placeholder for future credit-bureau + accounting joins. `status` is always `"not_available"` in v1; `credit_bureau_monthly_payment_total` and `accounting_debt_
payment_total` are `null`.

---

## `checklist` (underwriting-style gates)

| Key | Meaning |
|-----|---------|
| `no_unexplained_data_gaps_in_monthly_series` | `status`: no missing months inside the span; lists `missing_months`. |
| `net_cash_trend_reviewed_for_window` | `status`: at least 2 months observed. |
| `volatility_measured_and_interpreted` | Always `status: true`; echoes volatility percentages. |
| `negative_month_count_documented` | Always `true`; echoes `months_with_negative_flow`. |
| `concentration_risks_noted` | Always `true`; echoes top income/expense percentages. |
| `bounce_dishonor_nsf_signals_reviewed` | Always `true`; echoes `high_fee_count` and `nsf_overdraft_indicators`. |
| `bank_observed_debt_payments_reconciled_with_bureau_accounting` | Always `status: false`, `reason: reconciliation_not_available`. |

---

## Full snapshot

`plaid_analytics_snapshot` in the same Mongo document is the complete `PlaidAnalyticsResult.model_dump()` (all accounts, categories, income categories, etc.) fo
r debugging and future UI.

  most important signals for understanding credit health. They explain

def _filter_inquiries_last_2_years(
    inquiries: list[CreditInquiry],
) -> list[CreditInquiry]:
    """Return only inquiries from the last 2 years, sorted newest first."""
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=730)
    filtered = []
    for inq in inquiries:
        if not inq.date:
            continue
        try:
            inq_date = datetime.strptime(inq.date[:10], "%Y-%m-%d").replace(
                tzinfo=timezone.utc
            )
            if inq_date >= cutoff:
                filtered.append(inq)
        except (ValueError, IndexError):
            # Include if date is unparseable (better to show than hide)
            filtered.append(inq)
    # Sort newest first
    filtered.sort(key=lambda i: i.date, reverse=True)
    return filtered


def _count_raw_inquiries(raw_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
    """Count CREDIT_INQUIRY records in raw Array payload."""
    src = raw_payload.get("CREDIT_RESPONSE", raw_payload)
    inquiries = src.get("CREDIT_INQUIRY")
    if isinstance(inquiries, list):
        return len(inquiries)
    if isinstance(inquiries, dict):
        return 1
    return 0


def _count_raw_payment_patterns(raw_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """
    Count tradelines with payment pattern data and start dates.

    Returns:
      (pattern_data_count, pattern_start_date_count)
    """
    src = raw_payload.get("CREDIT_RESPONSE", raw_payload)
    liabilities = src.get("CREDIT_LIABILITY")
    if isinstance(liabilities, dict):
        rows = [liabilities]
    elif isinstance(liabilities, list):
        rows = liabilities
    else:
        rows = []

    pattern_data_count = 0
    pattern_start_date_count = 0
    for row in rows:
        if not isinstance(row, dict):
            continue
        pattern = row.get("_PAYMENT_PATTERN")
        if not isinstance(pattern, dict):
            continue
        if pattern.get("@_Data"):
            pattern_data_count += 1
        if pattern.get("@_StartDate"):
            pattern_start_date_count += 1

    return pattern_data_count, pattern_start_date_count


def _needs_detail_refresh(
    raw_payload: dict[str, Any],
    cached_result: CreditAnalyticsResult,
) -> bool:
    """
    Detect stale pre-computed analysis that predates inquiry/account detail fields.
    """
    raw_inquiry_count = _count_raw_inquiries(raw_payload)
    cached_inquiry_count = len(cached_result.inquiries)
    if raw_inquiry_count > cached_inquiry_count:
        return True

    raw_pattern_count, raw_pattern_start_count = _count_raw_payment_patterns(raw_payload)
    cached_pattern_count = sum(
        1 for tl in cached_result.tradelines
        if (tl.payment_pattern_data or tl.payment_history)
    )
    cached_pattern_start_count = sum(
        1 for tl in cached_result.tradelines
        if tl.payment_pattern_start_date
    )

    if raw_pattern_count > cached_pattern_count:
        return True
    if raw_pattern_start_count > cached_pattern_start_count:
        return True

    return False

def _compute_seasonality(monthly_income: list[float], month_names: list[str]) -> SeasonalityAnalysis | None:
    if len(monthly_income) < 6:
        return None

    avg = statistics.mean(monthly_income) if monthly_income else 0
    if avg == 0:
        return None

    indexed = list(zip(month_names, monthly_income))
    indexed_sorted = sorted(indexed, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

    peak = indexed_sorted[:3]
    trough = indexed_sorted[-3:]

    peak_avg = statistics.mean([p[1] for p in peak])
    trough_avg = statistics.mean([t[1] for t in trough])

    ratio = round(peak_avg / trough_avg, 2) if trough_avg > 0 else 0

    if ratio > 2.0:
        risk = "high"
    elif ratio > 1.5:
        risk = "moderate"
    else:
        risk = "low"
